questions.stem:
—______ you ______ your lunch yet?
—Yes. I ______ it just now.
questions.explanation: 第一空：问句中有yet（已经），这是现在完成时的标志性时间状语，结构为Have/Has+过去分词，主语是you，用Have；第二空：have的过去分词是had；第三空：答句中有just now（刚才），这是一般过去时的标志，所以用had。口诀：yet现完，just now过去。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The book ______ is on the desk was written by Lu Xun.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系词的选择。先行词是The book（书），指物，在从句中作主语，因此用which或that。who/whom指人，where指地点。技巧：先行词是物选which/that，是人选who/whom，地点选where。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The old building ______ down last year to make room for a new school.
questions.explanation: 本题考查被动语态。主语The old building（旧楼）是动作take down（拆除）的承受者，所以用被动语态；时间状语last year（去年）表明用一般过去时，因此结构为was/were+过去分词，主语是单数，用was taken。A、B是主动语态，D是一般现在时被动，时态不符。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—How long have you ______ here?
—Since 2019. But I ______ in Beijing for three years before I came here.
questions.explanation: 第一空：how long（多久）询问持续时间，与since/for连用，用现在完成时，have been here表示"待在这里"；第二空：before I came here（在我来这儿之前）是过去的过去，但此处描述的是过去的事实（在北京工作），用一般过去时worked即可，不需要用过去完成时。B选项第二空时态错误，C、D第一空不能用worked（work是短暂动词，不能与how long连用表示持续）。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: We should _______ our best to protect the environment.
questions.explanation: 本题考查固定搭配。try one's best是固定短语，意为"尽某人最大努力"。其他选项：make制作，do做，take拿，都不能与one's best构成此含义的固定搭配。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you add "un-" to the word "happy", you get a word with the ______ meaning.
questions.explanation: 本题考查构词法。前缀un-表示"不、无、相反"，如happy（开心的）→unhappy（不开心的），因此是相反的意思（opposite）。same相同的，similar相似的，special特别的，都不符合。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The weather was terrible — it rained heavily and the wind blew strongly. It was really _______ outside.
questions.explanation: 本题考查通过上下文猜测词义。根据前文terrible（糟糕的）、rained heavily（下大雨）、blew strongly（刮大风）这些负面描述，可以推断出天气很糟糕。awful意为"糟糕的、可怕的"，符合语境。sunny晴朗的，pleasant愉快的，calm平静的，都与前文描述矛盾。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental project called "Green Campus". All students were (8)______ to take part in it. The goal was to make our school cleaner and more beautiful.

First, we (9)______ into small groups. Each group had different tasks. Some students were responsible for collecting recyclable waste like paper and plastic bottles. Others (10)______ care of the school garden by watering plants and removing weeds.

During the project, I learned a lot about waste sorting. I never knew that an old T-shirt could be (11)______ into a shopping bag before. This experience made me realize that everyone can do something to protect our planet. Small actions can make a big (12)______.

The project not only improved our school environment but also taught us the importance of teamwork. We felt proud of what we had achieved together.
questions.explanation: 根据上下文，学校启动环保项目，应该是"鼓励"学生参加。encouraged（鼓励）符合语境。asked要求，语气较平淡；forced强迫，语气太重且负面；warned警告，不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental project called "Green Campus". All students were (8)______ to take part in it. The goal was to make our school cleaner and more beautiful.

First, we (9)______ into small groups. Each group had different tasks. Some students were responsible for collecting recyclable waste like paper and plastic bottles. Others (10)______ care of the school garden by watering plants and removing weeds.

During the project, I learned a lot about waste sorting. I never knew that an old T-shirt could be (11)______ into a shopping bag before. This experience made me realize that everyone can do something to protect our planet. Small actions can make a big (12)______.

The project not only improved our school environment but also taught us the importance of teamwork. We felt proud of what we had achieved together.
questions.explanation: divide into是固定搭配，意为"分成"。这里指我们被分成小组。cut into切碎，broke into闯入，fell into落入，都不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental project called "Green Campus". All students were (8)______ to take part in it. The goal was to make our school cleaner and more beautiful.

First, we (9)______ into small groups. Each group had different tasks. Some students were responsible for collecting recyclable waste like paper and plastic bottles. Others (10)______ care of the school garden by watering plants and removing weeds.

During the project, I learned a lot about waste sorting. I never knew that an old T-shirt could be (11)______ into a shopping bag before. This experience made me realize that everyone can do something to protect our planet. Small actions can make a big (12)______.

The project not only improved our school environment but also taught us the importance of teamwork. We felt proud of what we had achieved together.
questions.explanation: take care of是固定短语，意为"照顾、照料"。这里指其他学生照料学校花园。make制作，give给予，get得到，都不能与care of构成此含义的搭配。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental project called "Green Campus". All students were (8)______ to take part in it. The goal was to make our school cleaner and more beautiful.

First, we (9)______ into small groups. Each group had different tasks. Some students were responsible for collecting recyclable waste like paper and plastic bottles. Others (10)______ care of the school garden by watering plants and removing weeds.

During the project, I learned a lot about waste sorting. I never knew that an old T-shirt could be (11)______ into a shopping bag before. This experience made me realize that everyone can do something to protect our planet. Small actions can make a big (12)______.

The project not only improved our school environment but also taught us the importance of teamwork. We felt proud of what we had achieved together.
questions.explanation: turn into是固定短语，意为"变成"。这里指旧T恤可以被变成购物袋，符合废物利用的语境。divide into分成，break into闯入，cut into切碎，都不符合句意。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental project called "Green Campus". All students were (8)______ to take part in it. The goal was to make our school cleaner and more beautiful.

First, we (9)______ into small groups. Each group had different tasks. Some students were responsible for collecting recyclable waste like paper and plastic bottles. Others (10)______ care of the school garden by watering plants and removing weeds.

During the project, I learned a lot about waste sorting. I never knew that an old T-shirt could be (11)______ into a shopping bag before. This experience made me realize that everyone can do something to protect our planet. Small actions can make a big (12)______.

The project not only improved our school environment but also taught us the importance of teamwork. We felt proud of what we had achieved together.
questions.explanation: make a difference是固定搭配，意为"有影响、起作用"。这里指小行动可以产生大影响，是环保主题常用表达。make progress取得进步，make a decision做决定，make a promise许诺，都不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Do you often feel tired after using your smartphone for a long time? Many people don't realize that the "blue light" from screens can harm our eyes and affect our sleep.

Scientists have found that blue light can stop our bodies from producing melatonin (褪黑素), a chemical that helps us sleep. When we look at screens before bedtime, we may find it hard to fall asleep. This is why many doctors suggest avoiding screens at least one hour before sleep.

However, there are ways to protect ourselves. Many devices now have a "night mode" that reduces blue light. You can also try the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. This gives your eyes a rest.

Some apps can even track how much time you spend on your phone and remind you to take breaks. Remember, technology is helpful, but we need to use it wisely to stay healthy.
questions.stem: According to the passage, what can blue light do?
options: C. Stop our bodies from producing melatonin.
D. Make our eyes see better in the dark.
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据第二段第一句"Scientists have found that blue light can stop our bodies from producing melatonin"可知，蓝光会阻止身体产生褪黑素。A选项改善睡眠质量与原文相反；B选项帮助产生更多褪黑素与原文stop相反；D选项让眼睛在黑暗中看得更清楚文中未提及。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
Do you often feel tired after using your smartphone for a long time? Many people don't realize that the "blue light" from screens can harm our eyes and affect our sleep.

Scientists have found that blue light can stop our bodies from producing melatonin (褪黑素), a chemical that helps us sleep. When we look at screens before bedtime, we may find it hard to fall asleep. This is why many doctors suggest avoiding screens at least one hour before sleep.

However, there are ways to protect ourselves. Many devices now have a "night mode" that reduces blue light. You can also try the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. This gives your eyes a rest.

Some apps can even track how much time you spend on your phone and remind you to take breaks. Remember, technology is helpful, but we need to use it wisely to stay healthy.
questions.stem: What does the writer suggest readers do?
options: A. Stop using all electronic devices immediately.
B. Use night mode and take regular breaks.
C. Take medicine to help sleep better.
D. Only use phones during daytime.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据第三段，作者建议使用night mode（夜间模式）和20-20-20 rule（每20分钟休息20秒看20英尺外），即定期休息。A选项立即停止所有电子设备过于绝对；C选项吃药文中未提及；D选项只在白天使用手机过于绝对且文中未提及。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
Do you often feel tired after using your smartphone for a long time? Many people don't realize that the "blue light" from screens can harm our eyes and affect our sleep.

Scientists have found that blue light can stop our bodies from producing melatonin (褪黑素), a chemical that helps us sleep. When we look at screens before bedtime, we may find it hard to fall asleep. This is why many doctors suggest avoiding screens at least one hour before sleep.

However, there are ways to protect ourselves. Many devices now have a "night mode" that reduces blue light. You can also try the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. This gives your eyes a rest.

Some apps can even track how much time you spend on your phone and remind you to take breaks. Remember, technology is helpful, but we need to use it wisely to stay healthy.
questions.stem: The underlined word "track" in Paragraph 4 probably means _______.
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文，app可以track你在手机上花费的时间并提醒你休息，因此track意为"追踪、记录"。reduce减少，waste浪费，increase增加，都不符合语境。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
Do you often feel tired after using your smartphone for a long time? Many people don't realize that the "blue light" from screens can harm our eyes and affect our sleep.

Scientists have found that blue light can stop our bodies from producing melatonin (褪黑素), a chemical that helps us sleep. When we look at screens before bedtime, we may find it hard to fall asleep. This is why many doctors suggest avoiding screens at least one hour before sleep.

However, there are ways to protect ourselves. Many devices now have a "night mode" that reduces blue light. You can also try the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds. This gives your eyes a rest.

Some apps can even track how much time you spend on your phone and remind you to take breaks. Remember, technology is helpful, but we need to use it wisely to stay healthy.
questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: B. How to Make Better Use of Blue Light
C. The Dangers of Blue Light and How to Protect Ourselves
D. Why We Should Stop Using Technology
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章第一段介绍蓝光危害，第二段讲影响睡眠，第三、四段讲保护措施，因此最佳标题是"蓝光的危害及如何保护自己"。A选项智能手机的历史只在开头提及；B选项如何更好利用蓝光与文意相反；D选项为什么应该停止科技过于绝对，文中说科技有帮助但需要明智使用。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Many students find it difficult to balance study and rest. They often feel stressed when they have too much homework. (17)______ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a plan. Write down what you need to do each day. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important tasks.

Second, take regular breaks. Your brain needs rest to work well. (19)______ You can listen to music or do some exercise.

Finally, don't compare yourself with others too much. Everyone has their own pace. (20)______ Just do your best and stay positive.
options: A. However, with proper time management, you can handle it well.
B. For example, study for 45 minutes and then rest for 10 minutes.
C. In addition, checking off completed tasks gives you a sense of achievement.
D. As a result, you will feel more relaxed and perform better in school.
E. Remember that progress is more important than perfection.
questions.explanation: 前句说学生感到压力大，后句说"这里有一些有用的建议"，因此此处需要转折过渡，表示"然而，通过适当的时间管理，你可以处理好"。However表转折，承接前文的困难并引出下文的解决方案。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many students find it difficult to balance study and rest. They often feel stressed when they have too much homework. (17)______ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a plan. Write down what you need to do each day. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important tasks.

Second, take regular breaks. Your brain needs rest to work well. (19)______ You can listen to music or do some exercise.

Finally, don't compare yourself with others too much. Everyone has their own pace. (20)______ Just do your best and stay positive.
options: A. However, with proper time management, you can handle it well.
B. For example, study for 45 minutes and then rest for 10 minutes.
C. In addition, checking off completed tasks gives you a sense of achievement.
D. As a result, you will feel more relaxed and perform better in school.
E. Remember that progress is more important than perfection.
questions.explanation: 前句说"写下每天需要做的事"，此处应进一步说明这样做的好处，In addition（此外）表示递进，"核对完成的任务会给你成就感"符合制定计划后的逻辑。B是举例，但此处不需要举例；D是结果，但逻辑上不如C紧密。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many students find it difficult to balance study and rest. They often feel stressed when they have too much homework. (17)______ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a plan. Write down what you need to do each day. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important tasks.

Second, take regular breaks. Your brain needs rest to work well. (19)______ You can listen to music or do some exercise.

Finally, don't compare yourself with others too much. Everyone has their own pace. (20)______ Just do your best and stay positive.
options: A. However, with proper time management, you can handle it well.
B. For example, study for 45 minutes and then rest for 10 minutes.
C. In addition, checking off completed tasks gives you a sense of achievement.
D. As a result, you will feel more relaxed and perform better in school.
E. Remember that progress is more important than perfection.
questions.explanation: 前句说"定期休息"，此处需要举例说明如何休息，For example（例如）引出具休做法"学习45分钟休息10分钟"，后句"你可以听音乐或做运动"也是休息的方式，与B选项并列。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many students find it difficult to balance study and rest. They often feel stressed when they have too much homework. (17)______ Here are some useful tips.

First, make a plan. Write down what you need to do each day. (18)______ This way, you won't forget important tasks.

Second, take regular breaks. Your brain needs rest to work well. (19)______ You can listen to music or do some exercise.

Finally, don't compare yourself with others too much. Everyone has their own pace. (20)______ Just do your best and stay positive.
options: A. However, with proper time management, you can handle it well.
B. For example, study for 45 minutes and then rest for 10 minutes.
C. In addition, checking off completed tasks gives you a sense of achievement.
D. As a result, you will feel more relaxed and perform better in school.
E. Remember that progress is more important than perfection.
questions.explanation: 前句说"不要和别人比较太多，每个人都有自己的节奏"，此处应总结这个观点，"记住进步比完美更重要"符合语境，强调个人进步而非完美主义。Just do your best也呼应了progress的主题。
questions.label: 短文填空
